proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_osimertinib.

analyses.code: rate diff and univariable logistic regression of objective_response on treatment_pembrolizumab
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 17.4% on pembrolizumab vs 16.4% off (absolute diff +0.99 percentage points; logit OR 1.073).

analyses.code: rate diff and univariable logit of objective_response on treatment_sotorasib
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 16.9% on sotorasib vs 16.9% off (diff +0.08 pp; OR 1.006). No detectable main effect.

analyses.code: rate diff and univariable logit of objective_response on treatment_olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 17.0% on olaparib vs 16.9% off (diff +0.13 pp; OR 1.010). No detectable main effect.

analyses.code: rate diff and univariable logit of objective_response on treatment_osimertinib
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 16.5% on osimertinib vs 17.0% off (diff -0.50 pp; OR 0.965). Direction negative but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response more in patients with tmb_high=1 than in tmb_high=0 (positive interaction).
treatment_sotorasib increases objective_response more in patients with kras_g12c=1 than in kras_g12c=0 (positive interaction).
treatment_osimertinib increases objective_response more in patients with egfr_mutation=1 than in egfr_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
treatment_olaparib increases objective_response more in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in brca2_mutation=0 (positive interaction).

analyses.code: stratified rates and logit with treatment_pembrolizumab*tmb_high interaction
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect concentrated in TMB-high: 19.7% on vs 16.7% off (diff +2.95 pp, p=5.1e-6). In TMB-low: 16.5% vs 16.3% (diff +0.20 pp, NS). Interaction log-OR +0.184.

analyses.code: stratified rates and logit with treatment_sotorasib*kras_g12c interaction
analyses.result_summary: Among KRAS-G12C+: 17.5% on sotorasib vs 16.6% off (diff +0.81 pp, NS). Interaction log-OR +0.059, p=0.43. No biomarker-specific benefit detected.

analyses.code: stratified rates and logit with treatment_osimertinib*egfr_mutation interaction
analyses.result_summary: Among EGFR-mut: 16.4% on osimertinib vs 16.6% off (diff -0.20 pp, NS). Interaction log-OR +0.024, p=0.76. No biomarker-specific benefit detected.

analyses.code: stratified rates and logit with treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation interaction
analyses.result_summary: Among BRCA2-mut: 14.6% on olaparib vs 16.8% off (diff -2.16 pp, NS). Interaction log-OR -0.177, p=0.28. Point estimate negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
stage_iv=1 is associated with lower probability of objective_response than stage_iv=0.
has_brain_mets=1 is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l, nlr, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l are each associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.code: univariable logit objective_response ~ ecog_ps; rates by ecog stratum
analyses.result_summary: Response rate falls monotonically with ECOG: 21.1% (ECOG 0), 15.7% (ECOG 1), 11.1% (ECOG 2). Logit coef -0.375, OR 0.687 per unit.

analyses.code: Fisher exact for stage_iv vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Stage IV: 15.4% response vs 19.6% in non-stage IV (diff -4.25 pp).

analyses.code: Fisher exact for has_brain_mets vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Brain mets present: 14.4% response vs 17.7% absent (diff -3.39 pp).

analyses.code: univariable logit objective_response ~ albumin_g_dl
analyses.result_summary: Higher albumin associated with higher response; coef +0.0907, OR 1.095 per g/dL.

analyses.code: univariable logit objective_response ~ weight_loss_pct_6mo
analyses.result_summary: Greater weight loss associated with lower response; coef -0.0385, OR 0.962 per 1%-point of loss.

analyses.code: univariable logit objective_response ~ crp_mg_l
analyses.result_summary: Higher CRP associated with lower response; coef -0.0049 per mg/L.

analyses.code: univariable logits for ldh, nlr, alk phos, hemoglobin, calcium, platelets, wbc
analyses.result_summary: ldh_u_l (p=0.51), nlr (p=0.38), alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (p=0.19), hemoglobin (p=0.60), calcium (p=0.73), platelets (p=0.57), wbc (p=0.27) — no detectable univariable effect on response. Liver, bone, adrenal mets, pleural and pericardial effusions, and contralateral lung mets were also non-significant; only brain mets among site-of-disease variables affects response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stk11_mutation=1 reduces the magnitude of the treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response (negative interaction).
keap1_mutation=1 reduces the magnitude of the treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response (negative interaction).
tp53_mutation=1 modifies the magnitude of the treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response.
treatment_sotorasib has no benefit even within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup after restricting to that population (refined null).
treatment_osimertinib has no benefit even within the egfr_mutation=1 subgroup (refined null).
treatment_olaparib has no benefit, and may trend toward harm, within the brca2_mutation=1 subgroup (refined null).

analyses.code: stratified rates and logit with treatment_pembrolizumab*stk11_mutation interaction
analyses.result_summary: STK11 wt: pembro 17.7% vs 16.2% off (diff +1.42 pp, p=9.5e-5). STK11 mut: pembro 15.8% vs 17.3% off (diff -1.52 pp, p=0.08). Interaction log-OR -0.211 — STK11 mutation abrogates pembro benefit.

analyses.code: stratified rates and logit with treatment_pembrolizumab*keap1_mutation interaction
analyses.result_summary: KEAP1 wt: pembro 17.4% vs 16.3% (diff +1.12 pp, p=0.002). KEAP1 mut: pembro 17.1% vs 16.8% (diff +0.31 pp, NS). Interaction log-OR -0.058 (not significant).

analyses.code: stratified rates and logit with treatment_pembrolizumab*tp53_mutation interaction
analyses.result_summary: TP53 wt: pembro 17.3% vs 16.5% (diff +0.81 pp, p=0.07). TP53 mut: pembro 17.5% vs 16.3% (diff +1.20 pp, p=0.02). Interaction log-OR +0.028 (not significant).

analyses.code: subgroup analysis restricted to kras_g12c=1
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS-G12C+: response 17.5% on sotorasib vs 16.6% off (diff +0.81 pp). No clinically meaningful or statistically significant benefit.

analyses.code: subgroup analysis restricted to egfr_mutation=1
analyses.result_summary: Within EGFR-mut: 16.4% on osimertinib vs 16.6% off (diff -0.20 pp). No benefit.

analyses.code: subgroup analysis restricted to brca2_mutation=1
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA2-mut: 14.6% on olaparib vs 16.8% off (diff -2.16 pp). Trend toward worse response on olaparib but NS in this small subgroup (n=1519).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among treatment_pembrolizumab=1 patients, higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response (dose-response).
Among treatment_pembrolizumab=0 patients, pdl1_tps is not associated with objective_response (specificity check).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response increases with continuous pdl1_tps (positive multiplicative interaction on the logit scale).
treatment_pembrolizumab provides no measurable benefit when pdl1_tps is below 0.5 (PD-L1-low subgroup).

analyses.code: logit response ~ pdl1_tps in pembro-treated subset; quartile rates
analyses.result_summary: Within pembro-treated, response rises across PD-L1 quartiles: Q1 16.9%, Q2 16.2%, Q3 16.4%, Q4 20.1%. Coef +0.454 (OR 1.575 from PD-L1=0 to 1).

analyses.code: logit response ~ pdl1_tps in non-pembro subset
analyses.result_summary: Within non-pembro, PD-L1 not associated with response (coef -0.137, p=0.09; if anything trends opposite). Confirms PD-L1 acts as predictive (not prognostic) marker for pembro.

analyses.code: logit response ~ pembro + pdl1_tps + pembro*pdl1_tps
analyses.result_summary: Continuous interaction coef +0.591 (z=5.24). At PD-L1=0, pembro main effect is slightly negative (-0.114). Strong predictive marker pattern.

analyses.code: Fisher exact in pdl1_high=0 stratum (PD-L1<0.5)
analyses.result_summary: PD-L1-low: pembro 16.4% vs 16.6% off (diff -0.21 pp). No benefit detected in PD-L1-low patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Smoking_status modifies treatment_pembrolizumab benefit such that ever-smokers (current/former) derive larger benefit than never-smokers.
Histology (squamous vs adenocarcinoma) modifies treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response.
Smoking_pack_years is associated with objective_response (positively, reflecting mutational burden).

analyses.code: stratified rates by smoking_status and pembro; logit with smoking dummy interactions
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect by smoking: never +0.12 pp (p=0.90); former +1.08 pp (p=0.02); current +1.24 pp (p=0.05). Suggestive trend but interactions in 3-level logit are NS (inter_former p=0.33, inter_current p=0.31).

analyses.code: logit with squamous indicator and pembro*squamous interaction
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect ~similar across histology: adeno +0.86 pp (p=0.03), squamous +1.31 pp (p=0.04). Interaction log-OR +0.030, p=0.58. No meaningful histology modification.

analyses.code: univariable logit response ~ smoking_pack_years
analyses.result_summary: Pack-years not associated with response (coef -0.00015 per pack-year, OR ~1.00). Pack-years*pembro interaction also NS (-0.0011, p=0.27).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race_ethnicity is associated with objective_response (overall test of heterogeneity).
Insurance_type is associated with objective_response.
rural_residence=1 is associated with lower objective_response.
sex_female=1 is associated with higher objective_response than males.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
Receipt of each of the four treatments differs by race_ethnicity (treatment-allocation disparity).
Receipt of each of the four treatments differs by insurance_type (treatment-allocation disparity).

analyses.code: chi-square race_ethnicity x objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Borderline overall difference: rates white 16.5%, hispanic 17.2%, asian 17.7%, black 17.8%, other 18.5%. Chi-square p=0.045 — white slightly lower than other groups.

analyses.code: chi-square insurance_type x objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Rates: private 17.2%, medicare 16.8%, medicaid 16.1%, uninsured 17.0%. No significant overall difference.

analyses.code: Fisher exact rural_residence vs response
analyses.result_summary: Rural 16.7% vs urban 17.0% (diff -0.29 pp). NS.

analyses.code: Fisher exact sex_female vs response and adjusted multivariable confirmation
analyses.result_summary: Female 17.5% vs male 16.4% (diff +1.16 pp). Persists in MV-adjusted model (coef +0.081, p=0.001).

analyses.code: univariable logit response ~ age_years
analyses.result_summary: Coef +0.0005 per year of age, OR 1.0005 — essentially no effect.

analyses.code: chi-square treatment receipt by race_ethnicity
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab (p=0.43), sotorasib (p=0.11), osimertinib (p=0.64) — no allocation differences. Olaparib borderline (p=0.012, range 28.7%-32.2% across groups). Overall, treatment allocation appears largely race-blind in this dataset.

analyses.code: chi-square treatment receipt by insurance_type
analyses.result_summary: All four treatments NS: pembro p=0.27, sotorasib p=0.78, olaparib p=0.10, osimertinib p=0.91. No detectable insurance-based allocation disparities.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 23 SNP variants is significantly associated with objective_response after Bonferroni correction (alpha=0.05/23=0.00217).

Common comorbidities (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, CKD, HF, CAD, AF, VTE, autoimmune, hep B, hep C, HIV, prior malignancy, depression/anxiety, ILD) are individually associated with objective_response.
Higher symptom burden (fatigue, pain, dyspnea, cough, appetite loss grades) is associated with lower objective_response.
More prior lines of therapy is associated with lower objective_response, and prior_chemotherapy specifically lowers response.

analyses.code: Fisher exact for each of 23 snp_* columns vs objective_response; sort by p
analyses.result_summary: Best two SNPs: snp_rs7412 (rate +1.45 pp, p=0.020) and snp_rs1050828 (rate -1.35 pp, p=0.032). Neither survives Bonferroni at alpha=0.00217. Conclusion: no detectable SNP main effect on response.

analyses.code: Fisher exact for each comorbidity vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: All p>0.05 except coronary_artery_disease (rate -0.86 pp, p=0.049, borderline and not multiplicity-controlled). Diabetes, hypertension, COPD, CKD, HF, AF, VTE, autoimmune disease, hep B/C, HIV, prior malignancy, depression/anxiety, ILD all NS. No meaningful comorbidity signal.

analyses.code: univariable logit response ~ each symptom grade
analyses.result_summary: Fatigue_grade borderline (coef -0.022, p=0.040). Pain_nrs (p=0.78), dyspnea_grade (p=0.49), cough_grade (p=0.26), appetite_loss_grade (p=0.35) all NS. Symptom burden weakly negative at most; albumin/CRP/weight loss are stronger fingerprints of response.

analyses.code: Fisher exact for prior_* indicators; logit on prior_lines_of_therapy
analyses.result_summary: Prior chemo trends slightly negative (16.6% vs 17.2%, p=0.066, borderline). Prior radiation, surgery, immunotherapy, targeted therapy all NS. prior_lines_of_therapy coef -0.012 per line (p=0.24), no clear effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage, brain/liver/bone mets, key biomarkers, albumin, CRP, and weight loss, treatment_pembrolizumab still increases objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_sotorasib, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib still show no benefit on objective_response.
After adjustment plus interactions, the treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps and treatment_pembrolizumab x tmb_high interactions remain positive while pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation remains negative.
Sex_female remains independently positive for objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: multivariable logit response ~ demographics + biomarkers + labs + 4 treatments
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted pembro coef +0.076 (OR 1.079), p=0.002. Confirms small overall benefit persists after adjustment.

analyses.code: same multivariable logit, treatment coefficients for sotorasib/olaparib/osimertinib
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib coef +0.006 (p=0.83), olaparib +0.012 (p=0.64), osimertinib -0.035 (p=0.18). All adjusted treatment effects are null. None of the targeted agents shows benefit even after controlling for prognosis and biomarker imbalance.

analyses.code: multivariable logit adding pembro*pdl1_tps, pembro*stk11_mutation, pembro*tmb_high
analyses.result_summary: pembro x pdl1_tps log-OR +0.592 (p=2e-7); pembro x tmb_high +0.174 (p=9.5e-4); pembro x stk11 -0.204 (p=0.003). All three interactions retain direction and significance after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: sex_female coefficient in adjusted multivariable logit
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted sex_female log-OR +0.081 (OR 1.084), p=0.001. Independently associated with higher response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is largest in the joint subgroup pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1, i.e. PD-L1 and TMB act multiplicatively.
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response is essentially absent in stk11_mutation=1 patients regardless of PD-L1 status.
Within the 'best ICI candidate' triple subgroup (pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0), treatment_pembrolizumab markedly increases objective_response.
After adjustment for ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, and weight loss, treatment_sotorasib still has no effect on objective_response within kras_g12c=1.
After the same adjustment, treatment_osimertinib has no effect on objective_response within egfr_mutation=1.
After the same adjustment, treatment_olaparib has no effect (or trends harmful) on objective_response within brca2_mutation=1.

analyses.code: stratified Fisher exact in 4 cells of pdl1_high x tmb_high; pembro effect within each
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect by joint stratum: PD-L1-low/TMB-low diff -0.21 pp (NS), PD-L1-low/TMB-high -0.18 pp (NS), PD-L1-high/TMB-low +1.75 pp (p=0.04), PD-L1-high/TMB-high +13.86 pp (p=8e-21; 29.4% vs 15.5%). Striking multiplicative pattern — benefit concentrated when both markers are positive.

analyses.code: stratified Fisher in 4 cells of pdl1_high x stk11_mutation
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect: STK11wt/PD-L1low +0.07 pp (NS), STK11mut/PD-L1low -1.80 pp (NS), STK11wt/PD-L1high +6.37 pp (p=4e-15), STK11mut/PD-L1high -0.46 pp (NS). STK11 mutation eliminates the PD-L1-high pembro benefit. Confirmed by stratified continuous interaction: pembro x pdl1_tps coef +0.644 (p=1.3e-7) in STK11wt vs +0.256 (p=0.38) in STK11mut.

analyses.code: Fisher exact in PD-L1-high & TMB-high & STK11-WT subgroup (n=2714)
analyses.result_summary: 31.2% on pembro vs 15.3% off (absolute diff +15.96 pp). The clearest and largest treatment effect identified anywhere in the dataset.

analyses.code: logit response ~ sotorasib + ECOG + stage_iv + brain_mets + albumin + weight_loss in KRAS-G12C subset
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted sotorasib coef +0.057, OR 1.059 in KRAS-G12C subset (n=6368). NS. ECOG (-0.45), stage_iv (-0.34), brain_mets (-0.24), weight_loss (-0.04) dominate.

analyses.code: logit response ~ osimertinib + same covars in EGFR-mutant subset
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted osimertinib coef -0.013, OR 0.987 in EGFR-mut subset. NS. No detectable benefit even with adjustment.

analyses.code: logit response ~ olaparib + same covars in BRCA2-mutant subset
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib coef -0.159, OR 0.853 in BRCA2-mut subset (n=1519). Direction negative (trend toward harm), but NS.
